sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Lecce's form in the last 5 matches has been inconsistent, with only 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, struggling offensively with just 2 goals scored. Roma, on the other hand, have been more solid, securing 3 wins and 2 losses, showing better attacking output with 8 goals. This gives Roma a slight edge in terms of momentum heading into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 encounters, Roma have dominated Lecce, winning both matches (4-1 and 1-0). Historically, Roma are unbeaten in 9 games against Lecce, with 3 wins in their last 5 head-to-heads, suggesting a psychological advantage for the away side.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Lecce, sitting 16th with 17 points, have struggled offensively (12 goals, 19th in the league) and defensively (23 goals conceded). Roma, in 6th with 33 points, have a more balanced attack (20 goals) and defense (12 conceded), ranking higher in key metrics like possession and final third entries.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Lecce will miss key midfielders like Medon Berisha, potentially impacting their creativity. Roma's missing defenders like Gianluca Mancini could expose their backline, but players like Matías Soulé (5 goals, 3 assists) and in-form Evan Ferguson (3 goals in last 5) could exploit Lecce's defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Lecce's high tackle rate (2nd in the league) and defensive clearances (3rd) indicate a reliance on a compact, defensive setup, likely aiming to frustrate Roma. Roma's superior possession (59% in recent games) and ability to score late (33% of goals in 76-90 min) could test Lecce’s resilience in the latter stages.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Roma’s superior form, head-to-head dominance, and attacking threats, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore's 47% probability for an away victory. Lecce’s home struggles and Roma’s ability to capitalize on chances suggest a narrow win for the visitors, though a draw (35% probability) remains possible if Lecce defend stoutly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Roma lead Lecce 1-0 at the Via del Mare stadium. The lone goal came early in the match from Evan Ferguson, who capitalized on a chance in the 14th minute with a right-footed strike. Roma have been the dominant side overall, showing greater intent and quality in their attacking play. Lecce, on the other hand, have struggled to create meaningful opportunities and will need a significant improvement in the second half to turn the game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Roma have controlled the game with a higher share of possession and more effective entries into the final third. Their expected goals reflect a clear superiority in creating scoring chances, with one big chance created and a missed opportunity to extend their lead. Lecce, despite having a comparable number of final third entries, have failed to translate their efforts into dangerous situations, with no shots on target or big chances created. Defensively, Roma have also been more solid, winning a higher percentage of duels and aerial challenges compared to Lecce, who have shown vulnerability at the back and picked up a couple of yellow cards.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Lecce showing some early initiative, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, momentum quickly shifted to Roma around the midpoint of the first half, especially leading up to and following their goal, as they sustained a strong attacking spell. After conceding, Lecce managed brief periods of pressure, particularly midway through the half, but couldn’t maintain consistency. Roma regained control later in the half with another prolonged period of dominance, dictating the pace and threatening Lecce’s defense repeatedly before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and areas of concern
halftimeSections.text: For Roma, Evan Ferguson has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat with his positioning and finishing ability. Players like Zeki Çelik and Stephan El Shaarawy have contributed to Roma’s balanced approach on both ends of the pitch. Lecce, however, have lacked a cutting edge, with no player managing to test Roma’s goalkeeper significantly. Defensively, Lecce’s backline, despite efforts from players like Kialonda Gaspar and Tiago Gabriel with their clearances, has been under constant pressure. Additionally, disciplinary issues with yellow cards could limit their aggression in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Lecce will likely need to adopt a more aggressive approach in the second half, focusing on creating chances through quicker transitions and utilizing their wing play to exploit any gaps in Roma’s defense. They might also benefit from introducing fresh legs to inject energy and creativity in midfield. Roma, meanwhile, will aim to maintain their composure, manage the game with their possession-based style, and look for opportunities to counter-attack or capitalize on set-pieces. Their focus will be on tightening up defensively to protect their lead while seeking to exploit Lecce’s desperation if they push forward.
